$comment: A RECORD of hosted-database verification that was actually performed, so
deploy:check can reflect proven reality without contacting the hosted
project itself (it never does, by design).
This is a claim with a date and a method, not a live check. deploy:check
says so when it reads this file. If the hosted schema changes, this record
becomes wrong and must be re-verified — that is the cost of recording a
Never put keys, row contents, or user data in this file.

migrationsApplied.verifiedBy: schema query: last_active_at column and activate_birth_profile function present
migrationsApplied.rehearsed: twice against a scratch database built to the hosted schema

migrationsApplied.verifiedBy: schema query: ask_conversations and ask_messages present, RLS enabled, 8 policies
migrationsApplied.rehearsed: twice against a scratch database built to the hosted schema

rlsVerification.method: scripts/rls-check.js against the hosted project with two live disposable users
coverage: cross-user read, update, delete, and activate all denied
ownership cannot be forged on insert
cross-user Ask Orbit conversation access denied
anonymous callers receive no rows and cannot write
rlsVerification.cleanup: disposable users deleted; hosted row counts returned to pre-test values

dataIntegrity.note: Counts recorded before and after every hosted operation; existing accounts and their data unchanged.
